name: reverse-engineering-improvement-loop
description: Review completed reverse-engineering work, extract reusable lessons, and feed approved improvements back into workflows, skills, and persistent expert narratives.

session_artifact.description: Session log, report, evidence bundle, or workflow output to review

user_feedback.description: Optional operator feedback on what felt high-friction or high-value

apply_persona_changes.description: Whether approved persona updates should be written to persistent narratives

improvement_report.description: Prioritized improvements for workflows, skills, personas, and memory artifacts

inputs.instruction: Review the supplied session artifact as if it were the combined output of evidence-capture, session-replay, and operator notes. Extract recurring friction points, repeated unknowns, brittle handoffs, and automation opportunities.
Convene the Reverse Engineering Panel. Determine what the last session teaches about missing skills, weak workflow steps, overused tools, underused evidence, and memory leakage.

Apply the EVOKORE improvement-cycle methodology. Convert the reverse-engineering findings into concrete action items across workflow optimization, documentation, hook tuning, memory artifacts, and tooling additions.

Run the panel meta-improvement cycle for the Reverse Engineering Panel. Evaluate which personas added unique value, where expertise is still missing, and whether the challenge phase is producing genuine signal.
Produce a prioritized reverse-engineering improvement backlog. Separate fast documentation fixes, new skill candidates, new workflow assets, persona updates, and research spikes.
Write approved Reverse Engineering Panel narrative changes to the persistent narratives store and queue any workflow updates that still need user approval.
